## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On July 24, 1991, an absolutely massive, historically defining, and incredibly dramatic geopolitical and economic event occurred in the Indian Parliament. Finance Minister Manmohan Singh, heavily backed by Prime Minister P.V. Narasimha Rao, aggressively presented the historic 1991 Union Budget. Confronted by an absolute, catastrophic balance-of-payments crisis, Singh aggressively utilized this massive budget to officially and definitively dismantle the deeply oppressive "License Raj," fundamentally initiating the massive, aggressive neoliberal economic liberalization that saved and modernized the Indian republic.

On July 24, 1932, the Ramakrishna Mission Seva Pratishthan, an incredibly massive and highly vital general hospital, was officially and aggressively established in Calcutta by Swami Dayananda.
